benchmark_scope: Derive a minimal axiomatic framework A_1,...,A_k for quantum phases that: subsumes Landau-Ginzburg, classifies topological phases, handles gapless phases, produces computable invariants, and is falsifiable. The CGW path-connectivity definition only covers gapped phases; this target requires a strictly more general framework.
planning_constraints: Do not use the CGW path-connectivity definition as an axiom itself — it is what the framework must improve upon.
The framework MUST handle gapless phases; an axiom system covering only gapped phases is incomplete.
Landau order parameters are insufficient — the axioms must account for topological phases without symmetry breaking.
Do not assume all phases require a symmetry group; topological order exists without symmetry protection.
Each axiom must have an explicit falsification criterion — provide a hypothetical system that would violate it.
Invariants must have a defined computational procedure, even if computationally hard.
If a proposed axiom system fails to classify any of the four known phase types (symmetry-broken / topological-order / SPT / gapless-critical), mark it as refuted and propose a revision.
